questions.prompt: Select all that are true about what a transformer block is in this lesson.
choices: A same-shape editing layer for hidden-state rows
questions.explanation: A block receives an L by D hidden-state matrix and returns an L by D matrix with richer values. It is not the tokenizer vocabulary or the softmax table.

questions.prompt: Select all subparts that belong inside the block.
questions.explanation: Attention, MLP, residuals, and normalization are the mechanism pieces. Browser rendering is not part of the block.

questions.prompt: Select all statements that are true of the MLP inside a transformer block.
choices: It changes sequence length by itself
It directly stores the KV cache
questions.explanation: None are true. The MLP edits each row independently and keeps the hidden width shape.

questions.prompt: Order the block flow used in this lesson.
questions.explanation: That order preserves the old signal while adding context and per-row feature updates.

questions.prompt: Explain how attention and the MLP differ inside the transformer block.
questions.actual_answer: Attention mixes information across token positions, so one row can read from other rows. The MLP then works on each row independently, expanding and compressing features to create a per-token update. Both outputs are added back through residual paths, and the block returns the same L by D shape.
questions.rubric: A strong answer contrasts across-position mixing with per-row transformation, mentions residual addition, and says the output shape stays L by D.
questions.hint: Use across rows for attention and within a row for MLP.

questions.prompt: Select all that are true about what a transformer block passes to the next stage.
choices: It returns an updated hidden-state matrix
The output keeps the same L by D shape as the input
Each token row now carries more context-aware values
questions.explanation: All three are true. The block returns an updated hidden-state matrix of the same L by D shape, with each token row carrying more context-aware values.
